data.title: A new consortium for corporate climate action.
data.subtitle: The Climate Contribution Framework (CCF) was created to address a growing climate accountability gap: while companies increasingly disclose their emissions and transition plans, their overall contribution to global Net Zero remains fragmented, difficult to compare, and undervalued.

data.title: Co-initiated by Sweep and the Mirova Research Center.
data.subtitle: Together, these organizations combined climate science, financial research, and operational expertise to design a robust, transparent, and scalable methodology.

data.title: Sweep
data.subtitle: Leading platform for sustainability data management. Sweep supports large companies in collecting, analyzing, and communicating their climate data. Its technical infrastructure is at the heart of CCF integration into existing reporting systems.

data.title: Mirova Research Center
data.subtitle: Research branch of sustainable asset manager Mirova, the Research Center brings expertise in climate analysis, transition finance, and responsible investment. It ensures the academic and financial credibility of the CCF methodology.

data.contentText: Ans scientific development was carried out in collaboration with I Care by BearingPoint and Winrock International.

data.title: I Care by BearingPoint
data.subtitle: Leading sustainability strategy and environmental transition consultancy. I Care by BearingPoint brings deep expertise in low-carbon transition methodologies and ESG transformation. It contributes the strategic consulting frameworks that translate CCF methodology into actionable corporate pathways.

data.title: Winrock International.
data.subtitle: Global nonprofit leader in carbon market integrity and nature-based climate solutions. Winrock operates ACR, one of the world's first voluntary greenhouse gas registries, and pioneers science-based carbon crediting methodologies. Within the CCF, it ensures the rigor of carbon accounting and emissions verification standards.

data.title: Co-built with leading companies
data.subtitle: The framework was co-built with ten major companies representing diverse sectors and positions in the value chain. These companies provided their expertise, tested early versions of the methodology, and helped ensure its relevance to real corporate strategies.

data.title: An international group of experts to ensure scientific independence.
data.subtitle: The committee does not conduct company assessments and plays no commercial role in the scoring process.

data.title: World Business Council for Sustainable Development (WBCSD)
data.subtitle: Coalition of 200+ leading global companies committed to accelerating the transition to a sustainable world.

data.title: Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi)
data.subtitle: The global reference standard for validating emission reduction targets aligned with climate science.

data.title: CERES
data.subtitle: American non-profit organization mobilizing investors and companies on sustainability-related risks and opportunities.

data.title: International Carbon Reduction and Offset Alliance (ICROA)
data.subtitle: Governance body for voluntary carbon markets, guarantor of quality and integrity standards.

data.title: Exponential Roadmap Initiative
data.subtitle: Initiative aiming to accelerate climate solutions capable of halving global emissions by 2030 through exponential innovations.

data.title: World Benchmarking Alliance
data.subtitle: Alliance creating free public benchmarks to measure and stimulate corporate contribution to Sustainable Development Goals.

data.title: Stockholm Environment Institute
data.subtitle: Independent environmental research institute, international reference on climate policy, sustainable energy, and resource governance.

data.title: GHG Management Institute
data.subtitle: Institute specialized in training and research on measuring, managing, and reducing greenhouse gas emissions.

data.title: Environmental Defense Fund (EDF)
data.subtitle: Leading American environmental organization, pioneer in developing pragmatic solutions to global climate and environmental challenges.

data.title: A framework designed to be publicly accessible.
data.subtitle: Its development and dissemination are funded by philanthropy and sponsor contributions supporting methodological research — with no influence on scoring rules or results.

data.title: Want to explore your contribution // to global Net Zero?
data.subtitle: Join companies making visible what conventional assessments don't yet see.
